document.title: Risk Management Report for B Series

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for H&abyz B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW and FB1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk acceptability, residual risk evaluation, risk control verification, and production/post-production information activities for the device.
keyPoints: The device is a wired or wireless digital flat panel detector for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy.

The document lists applicable European Union and Republic of Korea regulations and relevant medical device, electrical safety, EMC, software, usability, biological evaluation, labeling, and radio standards.
Before risk control, 49 risks are summarized as 2 acceptable and 47 unacceptable; after risk control, all 49 risks are summarized as acceptable.
Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.

Production and post-production activities include customer satisfaction survey, complaint monitoring, inspection monitoring, design changes, standards monitoring, market information analysis, process monitoring, and purchaser assessment monitoring.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk (3.18)

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: risk management file set of records (3.16) and other documents that are produced by risk management (3.24)

concepts.name: Risk control
concepts.evidence: process (3.14) in which decisions are made and measures implemented by which risks (3.18) are reduced to, or maintained within, specified levels

concepts.name: Residual risk
concepts.evidence: risk remaining after risk control (3.21) measures have been implemented

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Intended purpose
concepts.evidence: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

evidence.claim: The document is a Risk Management Report for B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models B1417MCW, B1717MCW, FB1417MCW and FB1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Report for B Series Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Model name: B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.0 and the establishment date is Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date Oct/18/2024 Revision Description Establishment

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on Oct 18, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by R&D team Kim, Myeong seop Oct 18, 2024 Reviewed by R&D Manager Lee, Dong ho Oct 18, 2024 Approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu Oct 18, 2024

evidence.claim: The applicable national and regional regulations listed are EU Regulation 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Applicable national and regional regulations 1 European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 2 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The device classification stated in the report is IIa.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIa

evidence.claim: The expected service life stated in the report is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The B1417MCW/B1717MCW/FB1417MCW/FB1717MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide real-time precise case diagnosis and treatment planning.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnosis and treatment planning on a real time basis for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The detector supports auto exposure detection technology without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.

evidence.claim: The detector consists of TFT, CsI scintillator, and electronics that convert read data into digital signals.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector consists of TFT and Scintillator (CsI) and an electronic device that converts data read through it into digital signals.

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: B Series Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: Patient contact with the front of the detector is less than 10 minutes.
evidence.evidenceText: Patient contacts the front of the detector in less than 10 minutes.

evidence.claim: The product contains software.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.19 Does the medical device contain software? A The product contains software.

evidence.claim: The detector can connect to a PC via Wi-Fi.
evidence.evidenceText: The detector can be connected to a PC via Wi-Fi.

evidence.claim: The product does not store data.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.21 Does the medical device store data critical to patient care? N/A This product does not store data.

evidence.claim: The device is designed as portable.
evidence.evidenceText: A.2.34 Is the medical device intended to be mobile or portable? A Intended to be designed portable.

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 49 risks were summarized with 2 acceptable and 47 unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Table 2. Risk evaluation results before risk control ... EN ISO 14971:2019 2 47 49

evidence.claim: The report also states that 43 risks were identified and estimated, which conflicts with the 49-risk tables.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated.

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 49 risks were in the acceptable region and none were in the unacceptable region.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk evaluation results after risk control (Numbers in the regions) ... EN ISO 14971:2019 49 0 49

evidence.claim: All identified residual risks after risk control were evaluated as acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: Risk control measures included labeling and user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, different cable type design, IEC 62304 software development process, radio testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks are determined acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information should be obtained through A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-10-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting and reviewing production and post-production information and managing quality problems.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: Risk analysis for collected production and post-production information is performed once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager shall report safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata signals FDA, but the source text lists European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Republic of Korea Medical Device Act; no FDA authority text was found.
Risk count evidence is conflicting: the report states 43 risks were identified and estimated, while the before/after risk evaluation tables total 49 risks.
The source includes apparent OCR or document-quality issues, including inconsistent wording and likely typo references such as EN 15322-1 where EN 15223-1 appears elsewhere.
ProductSignal metadata is Unclassified even though the source identifies Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models and states Classification IIa.
